rationale.root_cause: They pinpointed program.c:12 using strcpy to copy argv[1] into buf[256], explaining that user-controlled command-line input can exceed the buffer, causing a stack buffer overflow.
rationale.local_fix: They only replaced strcpy with strncpy and added manual NUL termination, but did not reject overlong input before copying as required.
rationale.global_fix: They recommended removing unbounded string handling by using bounded string APIs (e.g., strlcpy/snprintf) and validating lengths before copying, which satisfies the root-cause criterion.
